title: System Design — E-commerce Order Service
learningObjectives: Defend **E-commerce order service design** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why System Design — E-commerce Order Service matters
sections.content: **Orders** touch **money** and **stock**. Interviewers listen for **sagas**, **idempotency**, **inventory** **holds**, and **honest** **2PC** limits.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - System Design — E-commerce Order Service
sections.content:
### Day 84 — **Order** **service**

### 1. Flow
**Cart** -> **checkout** -> **pay** -> **fulfill**.

### 2. Consistency
**Per-service** **transactions** + **saga**.

### 3. Inventory
**Reservation**, **oversell** **guards**.

### 4. Payments
**PSP** **callbacks**, **reconciliation**.

### 5. Failure
**Compensations**, **manual** **review** queues.

### 6. Story
**Payment** **succeeds**, **inventory** **fails**—**compensating** **refund** **saga** **step**.

sections.title: Basic — Core services
Intermediate — Saga steps
sections.output: reserve -> charge -> mark paid -> enqueue fulfillment

sections.title: Advanced — 2PC vs saga
Checkout saga
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 84 — Saga
Order -> Inventory : reserve
Inventory --> Order : ok
Order -> Payment : charge
Payment --> Order : fail
Order -> Inventory : release
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — System Design — E-commerce Order Service (Day 84 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: saga: loose coupling; 2PC: strong consistency, not always available

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Saga** **orchestrated** vs **choreography**?
conceptual.answer: Central coordinator vs event-driven peers—debuggability vs coupling trade-offs. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **E-commerce order service design**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Central coordinator vs event-driven peers—debuggability vs coupling trade-offs. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Central coordinator vs event-driven peers—debuggability vs coupling trade-offs. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

conceptual.question: **Inventory** **reservation** **pattern**?
conceptual.answer: Hold stock with TTL; confirm on payment; release on timeout. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **E-commerce order service design**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Hold stock with TTL; confirm on payment; release on timeout. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

conceptual.question: **Payment** **idempotency**?
conceptual.answer: Merchant key + gateway idempotency tokens to survive retries. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: **Order** **state** **machine**?
conceptual.answer: Placed, paid, packed, shipped—explicit transitions and side effects. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Piece | Recall |
|---|---|
| Saga | compensate |
| 2PC | blocking |
| Hold | TTL release |
| Idempotency | keys |
| Outbox | reliable events |
| PSP | webhooks |
| State machine | explicit |
| Stock | optimistic lock |
| Fulfill | async workers |
| Support | replay DLQ |
